Use Python to inspect the evidence, refine that world model from the newest history, and search or score candidate actions or short sequences against the current goal as you currently understand it.

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Interesting! When I moved the character RIGHT to cols 44-45 and then clicked on the green block at (27, 15), the green count went from 14 to 13. So clicking on a green block removed it!
But wait, the character was at cols 44-45 (right side) and I clicked on the green block at col 15 (left side). The green block was removed. This suggests that:
1. The character needs to be at a specific position (right side?) to enable clicking on green blocks
2. Clicking on green blocks removes them when the character is in the right position
Let me continue clicking on the remaining green blocks to remove them all. Then I need to figure out what to do next.
Looking at the image, I can see the character is now at the right side of the bottom blue region. The green blocks are still there but one was removed.
Let me continue clicking on the remaining green blocks. I need to click on 13 more green blocks.
But wait, I need to be careful about the efficiency. In Level 1, I clicked on green blocks one by one. Maybe there's a faster way?
Let me first check the current state and then continue clicking on green blocks.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# SUCCESS! Clicking on green block removed it (14 -> 13)
# Character is at cols 44-45 (right side)
# Need to continue removing green blocks

seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("Green blocks:")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['color'] == 'N':
        b = n['boundary']
        rows = [p[0] for p in b]
        cols = [p[1] for p in b]
        print(f"  rows {min(rows)}-{max(rows)}, cols {min(cols)}-{max(cols)}")

# Click on remaining green blocks
# Green blocks are at cols 13-17, 19-23, 25-29, 31-35, 37-41, 43-47, 49-53
# Rows 25-29 and 31-35
# One block at (25-29, 13-17) was removed
# Need to click on remaining 13 blocks

# Click on center of each remaining green block
clicks = [
    (27, 21), (27, 27), (27, 33), (27, 39), (27, 45), (27, 51),  # top row
    (33, 15), (33, 21), (33, 27), (33, 33), (33, 39), (33, 45), (33, 51)  # bottom row
]

for r, c in clicks:
    result = action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': r, 'col': c}])
    green_count = sum(1 for n in current_frame.segmentation['nodes'] if n['color'] == 'N')
    print(f"Click ({r},{c}): green={green_count}, gameplay_changed={last_action_result.get('gameplay_changed')}")
    if last_action_result.get('game_over') or last_action_result.get('level_completed'):
        break

print(f"\nFinal: level_completed={last_action_result.get('level_completed')}, game_over={last_action_result.get('game_over')}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
